Promotion/relegation play-off won by [AMEF Arad](/wiki/AMEF_Arad "AMEF Arad") (last place [1934–35 Divizia A](/wiki/1934%E2%80%9335_Divizia_A "1934–35 Divizia A")).

Victoria Constanța [ILSA Timișoara](/wiki/ILSA_Timi%C8%99oara "ILSA Timișoara") [Phoenix Baia Mare](/wiki/Phoenix_Baia_Mare "Phoenix Baia Mare") [IAR Brașov](/wiki/Tractorul_Bra%C8%99ov "Tractorul Brașov") [Franco-Româna Brăila](/wiki/Franco-Rom%C3%A2na_Br%C4%83ila "Franco-Româna Brăila") | [CFR Brașov](/wiki/AS_CFR_Bra%C8%99ov "AS CFR Brașov") Rovine Grivița Craiova [Olimpia CFR Satu Mare](/wiki/FC_Olimpia_Satu_Mare "FC Olimpia Satu Mare") [Șoimii Sibiu](/wiki/%C8%98oimii_Sibiu "Șoimii Sibiu") [Dacia Unirea Brăila](/wiki/ACS_Dacia_Unirea_Br%C4%83ila "ACS Dacia Unirea Brăila") | *None* | League play-off won by [ILSA Timișoara](/wiki/ILSA_Timi%C8%99oara "ILSA Timișoara"). Promotion/relegation play-off won by [Universitatea Cluj](/wiki/FC_Universitatea_Cluj "FC Universitatea Cluj") (last place [1935–36 Divizia A](/wiki/1935%E2%80%9336_Divizia_A "1935–36 Divizia A")).

**[Sportul Studențesc București](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București")** **[Phoenix Baia Mare](/wiki/Phoenix_Baia_Mare "Phoenix Baia Mare")** | **[CFR Brașov](/wiki/AS_CFR_Bra%C8%99ov "AS CFR Brașov")** **[Jiul Petroșani](/wiki/Jiul_Petro%C8%99ani "Jiul Petroșani")** | [Dacia Unirea Brăila](/wiki/ACS_Dacia_Unirea_Br%C4%83ila "ACS Dacia Unirea Brăila") (3rd) [Vulturii Textila Lugoj](/wiki/Vulturii_Textila_Lugoj "Vulturii Textila Lugoj") (3rd) | Six teams were promoted, and no teams were relegated. [1937–38 Divizia A](/wiki/1937%E2%80%9338_Divizia_A "1937–38 Divizia A") was expanded to 20 teams (two groups of 10).

**[Tricolor Ploiești](/wiki/FC_Ploie%C8%99ti "FC Ploiești")** **[UD Reșița](/wiki/FCM_Re%C8%99i%C8%9Ba "FCM Reșița")** | Victoria Constanța [CAM Timișoara](/wiki/CAM_Timi%C8%99oara "CAM Timișoara") | *None* | Two teams were promoted, and 10 teams were relegated. [1938–39 Divizia A](/wiki/1938%E2%80%9339_Divizia_A "1938–39 Divizia A") reverted to 12 Teams.

**[Unirea Tricolor București](/wiki/Unirea_Tricolor_Bucure%C8%99ti "Unirea Tricolor București")** **[Gloria CFR Galați](/wiki/Gloria_CFR_Gala%C8%9Bi "Gloria CFR Galați")** **[CAM Timișoara](/wiki/CAM_Timi%C8%99oara "CAM Timișoara")** [Mureșul Târgu Mureș](/wiki/Mure%C8%99ul_T%C3%A2rgu_Mure%C8%99 "Mureșul Târgu Mureș") | [Maccabi București](/wiki/Maccabi_Bucure%C8%99ti "Maccabi București") [Dacia VA Galați](/wiki/Dacia_Vasile_Alecsandri_Gala%C8%9Bi "Dacia Vasile Alecsandri Galați") [CA Oradea](/wiki/CA_Oradea "CA Oradea") [IS Câmpia Turzii](/wiki/CSM_C%C3%A2mpia_Turzii "CSM Câmpia Turzii") | *None* | [Mureșul Târgu Mureș](/wiki/Mure%C8%99ul_T%C3%A2rgu_Mure%C8%99 "Mureșul Târgu Mureș") lost in the promotion play-off, and the other three winners were promoted. 3 teams were relegated.

**[FC Ploiești](/wiki/FC_Ploie%C8%99ti "FC Ploiești")** **[Mica Brad](/wiki/Aurul_Brad "Aurul Brad")** [Crișana Oradea](/wiki/Cri%C8%99ana_Oradea "Crișana Oradea") [Franco-Româna Brăila](/wiki/Franco-Rom%C3%A2na_Br%C4%83ila "Franco-Româna Brăila") | [Mureșul Târgu Mureș](/wiki/Mure%C8%99ul_T%C3%A2rgu_Mure%C8%99 "Mureșul Târgu Mureș") [Minerul Lupeni](/wiki/CS_Minerul_Lupeni "CS Minerul Lupeni") **[Universitatea Cluj](/wiki/FC_Universitatea_Cluj "FC Universitatea Cluj")** Feroemail Ploiești | [FC Craiova](/wiki/FC_Craiova "FC Craiova") (3rd) [FC Brăila](/wiki/ACS_Dacia_Unirea_Br%C4%83ila "ACS Dacia Unirea Brăila") (4th) [Gloria Arad](/wiki/CS_Gloria_Arad "CS Gloria Arad") (6th) | *Workers' teams were banned*. [FC Brăila](/wiki/ACS_Dacia_Unirea_Br%C4%83ila "ACS Dacia Unirea Brăila") replaced [Franco-Româna Brăila](/wiki/Franco-Rom%C3%A2na_Br%C4%83ila "Franco-Româna Brăila"). [FC Craiova](/wiki/FC_Craiova "FC Craiova") and [Gloria Arad](/wiki/CS_Gloria_Arad "CS Gloria Arad") replaced [CAM Timișoara](/wiki/CAM_Timi%C8%99oara "CAM Timișoara") and [AMEF Arad](/wiki/AMEF_Arad "AMEF Arad"). [Crișana Oradea](/wiki/Cri%C8%99ana_Oradea "Crișana Oradea") from [Oradea](/wiki/Oradea "Oradea") was under [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") occupation.

*Two unofficial editions were played due to [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").* | | |

The **Liga 2**, most commonly spelled as **Liga II**, is the second level of the [Romanian football league system](/wiki/Romanian_football_league_system "Romanian football league system"). The league changed its name from **Divizia B** just before the start of the [2006–07](/wiki/2006%E2%80%9307_Liga_II "2006–07 Liga II") football season. It is currently [sponsored](/wiki/Sponsor_(commercial) "Sponsor (commercial)") by Casa Pariurilor, a betting company under the official name **Liga 2 Casa Pariurilor**.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Liga_II&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

Since its inception in 1934, Liga II has had between 2 and 9 parallel divisions, with clubs divided based on geographic regions. But since the [2016-17 Liga II](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Liga_II "2016–17 Liga II"), it changed to one group of 20 teams. Currently, the top six teams goes in the promotion play-off, in which the top 2 teams get promoted and the next 2 play a promotion play-off against teams from Liga I. In the play-out, there are 2 groups, 7th, 10th, 11th, etc. in group 1, 8th, 9th, 12th, etc. in group 2. the bottom 2 teams from each group gets relegated and the 3rd worst places in the 2 groups play each other home and away to decide the last team relegated.

### New format from 2024

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Liga_II&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: New format from 2024")]

On 14 July 2023, the federation announced that the league will be expanded to 22 teams starting with the 2024–2025 season.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The expansion will be achieved by having only 4 teams relegate at the end of the [2023-24 Liga II](/wiki/2023-24_Liga_II "2023-24 Liga II") season ( by giving up on the extra relegation match-up between the second to last teams in the play-out groups ), and having 6 teams promote from [Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") ( 5 play-off winners plus a sixth team to be determined by extra matches between the best 4 teams that lost in the final round of the Liga III play-off ).

The new format will be similar to the current one, with teams playing each other once in the regular season ( for a total of 21 match days this time ), followed by a promotion play-off with 6 teams, playing each other twice, and a relegation play-out with two groups of 8 teams playing each other once ( as opposed to the current play-out format with groups of 7 also playing each other once ).

On the promotion end, the format will be the same as currently, with the top two teams from the play-off promoting directly and next two playing an extra promotion play-off against teams from [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I").
